user.title: Choose a name for the Adaptive Lighting instance
user.description: Every instance can contain multiple lights!

menu.title: Create or Duplicate
menu.description: Do you want to create a new instance or duplicate an existing one?

init.title: Essentials
init.description: Pick your lights and set brightness and color ranges. Most users only need this page. For interactive graphs, visit [this web app](https://basnijholt.github.io/adaptive-lighting).

data.lights: lights: List of light entity_ids to be controlled (may be empty). 🌟
data.min_color_temp: min_color_temp: Warmest color temperature in Kelvin. 🔥
data.max_color_temp: max_color_temp: Coldest color temperature in Kelvin. ❄️
data.prefer_rgb_color: prefer_rgb_color: Whether to prefer RGB color adjustment over light color temperature when possible. 🌈
data.transition_until_sleep: transition_until_sleep: When enabled, Adaptive Lighting will treat sleep settings as the minimum, transitioning to these values after sunset. 🌙
data.take_over_control: take_over_control: Pause adaptation of individual lights and hand over (manual) control to other sources that issue `light.turn_on` calls for lights that are on. 🔒
data.detect_non_ha_changes: detect_non_ha_changes: Detects and halts adaptations for non-`light.turn_on` state changes. Needs `take_over_control` enabled. 🕵️ Caution: ⚠️ Some lights might falsely indicate an 'on' state, which could result in lights turning on unexpectedly. Note that this calls `homeassistant.update_entity` every `interval`! Disable this feature if you encounter such issues.
data.only_once: only_once: Adapt lights only when they are turned on (`true`) or keep adapting them (`false`). 🔄
data.adapt_only_on_bare_turn_on: adapt_only_on_bare_turn_on: When turning lights on initially. If set to `true`, AL adapts only if `light.turn_on` is invoked without specifying color or brightness. ❌🌈 This e.g., prevents adaptation when activating a scene and marks the light as manually controlled. If `false`, AL adapts regardless of the presence of color or brightness in the initial `service_data`. Needs `take_over_control` enabled. 🕵️
data.separate_turn_on_commands: separate_turn_on_commands: Use separate `light.turn_on` calls for color and brightness, needed for some light types. 🔀
data.skip_redundant_commands: skip_redundant_commands: Skip sending adaptation commands whose target state already equals the light's known state. Minimizes network traffic and improves the adaptation responsivity in some situations. 📉Disable if physical light states get out of sync with HA's recorded state.
data.intercept: intercept: Intercept and adapt `light.turn_on` calls to enabling instantaneous color and brightness adaptation. 🏎️ Disable for lights that do not support `light.turn_on` with color and brightness.
data.multi_light_intercept: multi_light_intercept: Intercept and adapt `light.turn_on` calls that target multiple lights. ➗⚠️ This might result in splitting up a single `light.turn_on` call into multiple calls, e.g., when lights are in different switches. Requires `intercept` to be enabled.
data.include_config_in_attributes: include_config_in_attributes: Show all options as attributes on the switch in Home Assistant when set to `true`. 📝
data.enable_diagnostic_sensors: enable_diagnostic_sensors: Expose per-light Adaptive Lighting status sensors when set to `true`. 🧭

data_description.interval: Frequency to adapt the lights, in seconds. 🔄
data_description.transition: Duration of transition when lights change, in seconds. 🕑
data_description.initial_transition: Duration of the first transition when lights turn from `off` to `on` in seconds. ⏲️
data_description.sleep_brightness: Brightness percentage of lights in sleep mode. Set to 0 to prevent lights from turning on. 😴
data_description.sleep_rgb_or_color_temp: Use either `"rgb_color"` or `"color_temp"` in sleep mode. 🌙
data_description.sleep_color_temp: Color temperature in sleep mode (used when `sleep_rgb_or_color_temp` is `color_temp`) in Kelvin. 😴
data_description.sleep_rgb_color: RGB color in sleep mode (used when `sleep_rgb_or_color_temp` is "rgb_color"). 🌈
data_description.sleep_transition: Duration of transition when "sleep mode" is toggled in seconds. 😴
data_description.sunrise_time: Set a fixed time (HH:MM:SS) for sunrise. 🌅
data_description.min_sunrise_time: Set the earliest virtual sunrise time (HH:MM:SS), allowing for later sunrises. 🌅
data_description.max_sunrise_time: Set the latest virtual sunrise time (HH:MM:SS), allowing for earlier sunrises. 🌅
data_description.sunrise_offset: Adjust sunrise time with a positive or negative offset in seconds. ⏰
data_description.sunset_time: Set a fixed time (HH:MM:SS) for sunset. 🌇
data_description.min_sunset_time: Set the earliest virtual sunset time (HH:MM:SS), allowing for later sunsets. 🌇
data_description.max_sunset_time: Set the latest virtual sunset time (HH:MM:SS), allowing for earlier sunsets. 🌇
data_description.sunset_offset: Adjust sunset time with a positive or negative offset in seconds. ⏰
data_description.brightness_mode: Brightness mode to use. Possible values are `default`, `linear`, and `tanh` (uses `brightness_mode_time_dark` and `brightness_mode_time_light`). 📈
data_description.brightness_mode_time_dark: (Ignored if `brightness_mode='default'`) The duration in seconds to ramp up/down the brightness before/after sunrise/sunset. 📈📉
data_description.brightness_mode_time_light: (Ignored if `brightness_mode='default'`) The duration in seconds to ramp up/down the brightness after/before sunrise/sunset. 📈📉.
data_description.take_over_control_mode: The adaptation pausing mode when other sources change brightness and/or color of lights. `pause_all` always pauses both brightness and color adaptation. `pause_changed` pauses the adaptation of only the changed attributes and continues adapting unchanged attributes, e.g., continues color adaptation when only brightness was changed.
data_description.autoreset_control_seconds: Automatically reset the manual control after a number of seconds. Set to 0 to disable. ⏲️
data_description.send_split_delay: Delay (ms) between `separate_turn_on_commands` for lights that don't support simultaneous brightness and color setting. ⏲️
data_description.adapt_delay: Wait time (seconds) between light turn on and Adaptive Lighting applying changes. Might help to avoid flickering. ⏲️

sleep.title: Sleep Mode
sleep.description: Configure how lights behave when sleep mode is active.

data_description.sleep_brightness: Brightness (%) applied while sleep mode is active.
data_description.sleep_rgb_or_color_temp: Selects whether sleep mode applies a color temperature (`color_temp`) or an RGB color (`rgb_color`).
data_description.sleep_color_temp: Color temperature (Kelvin) applied while sleep mode is active. Requires `sleep_rgb_or_color_temp` set to `color_temp`.
data_description.sleep_rgb_color: RGB color applied while sleep mode is active. Requires `sleep_rgb_or_color_temp` set to `rgb_color`.
data_description.sleep_transition: Fade duration (seconds) when entering or exiting sleep mode.
data_description.transition_until_sleep: Treats sleep mode values as the nighttime floor. After sunset, brightness and color temperature gradually approach the sleep settings instead of stopping at the configured minimums.

sun_timing.title: Sun & Timing
sun_timing.description: Fine-tune sunrise/sunset times, update intervals, and brightness curves.

data_description.sunrise_time: Overrides the calculated sunrise with a fixed time (HH:MM:SS). Leave unset to use the actual local sunrise.
data_description.min_sunrise_time: Earliest allowed sunrise time (HH:MM:SS). The effective sunrise never occurs before this time.
data_description.max_sunrise_time: Latest allowed sunrise time (HH:MM:SS). The effective sunrise never occurs after this time.
data_description.sunrise_offset: Shifts the sunrise time (seconds). Positive values delay sunrise; negative values advance it.
data_description.sunset_time: Overrides the calculated sunset with a fixed time (HH:MM:SS). Leave unset to use the actual local sunset.
data_description.min_sunset_time: Earliest allowed sunset time (HH:MM:SS). The effective sunset never occurs before this time.
data_description.max_sunset_time: Latest allowed sunset time (HH:MM:SS). The effective sunset never occurs after this time.
data_description.sunset_offset: Shifts the sunset time (seconds). Positive values delay sunset; negative values advance it.
data_description.interval: Update frequency (seconds) for recalculating and applying light settings.
data_description.initial_transition: Fade duration (seconds) when a light first turns on from an off state.
data_description.adapt_delay: Wait time (seconds) after a light turns on before applying adaptive settings. Increase if lights flicker or reset on turn-on.
data_description.brightness_mode: Brightness calculation algorithm. `default` follows a smooth solar curve. `linear` ramps evenly between min and max. `tanh` applies a gradual S-curve shaped by `brightness_mode_time_dark` and `brightness_mode_time_light`.
data_description.brightness_mode_time_dark: Dark-side transition window (seconds): how long before sunrise brightness begins rising and how long after sunset it finishes falling. Requires `brightness_mode` set to `linear` or `tanh`.
data_description.brightness_mode_time_light: Light-side transition window (seconds): how long after sunrise brightness finishes rising and how long before sunset it begins falling. Requires `brightness_mode` set to `linear` or `tanh`.

manual_control.title: Behavior
manual_control.description: How the integration responds when something else changes your lights.

data_description.take_over_control: Pauses adaptation when another source (automation, scene, or voice assistant) changes a light. The light stays paused until turned off and on again or reset via the `set_manual_control` service.
data_description.take_over_control_mode: Controls which attributes pause when another source changes a light. `pause_all` stops both brightness and color adaptation. `pause_changed` stops only the attribute that was changed and continues adapting the other.
data_description.detect_non_ha_changes: Detects changes made outside Home Assistant (physical switches, third-party apps) by polling lights each interval, and pauses adaptation until manually reset. Requires `take_over_control`. ⚠️ Some lights falsely report an 'on' state, which can cause them to turn on unexpectedly. Disable if you observe unintended behavior.
data_description.autoreset_control_seconds: Resumes adaptive control automatically after a light has been manually controlled for this duration (seconds). Set to 0 to disable auto-reset.
data_description.only_once: Applies adaptive settings only at the moment a light turns on, without continuing to update while the light stays on.
data_description.adapt_only_on_bare_turn_on: Skips adaptation when a light is turned on with explicit color or brightness values (e.g., a scene). The light is then treated as manually controlled. Requires `take_over_control`.
data_description.intercept: Prevents lights from flashing at incorrect settings on turn-on by intercepting the command and immediately applying adaptive brightness and color. Disable for lights that do not accept color or brightness on turn-on.
data_description.multi_light_intercept: Extends interception to commands targeting multiple lights at once. ⚠️ May split a single command into per-light calls, which can behave differently for lights managed by separate switches. Requires `intercept`.
data_description.skip_redundant_commands: Skips commands when the target state matches the light's last known state, reducing network traffic. Disable if light states drift out of sync.

workarounds.title: Device Workarounds
workarounds.description: Fixes for specific hardware quirks. Skip this page unless something isn't working right.

data_description.separate_turn_on_commands: Sends brightness and color as two separate commands instead of one. Required for light types that cannot handle both attributes in a single call.
data_description.send_split_delay: Delay (milliseconds) between the brightness and color commands. Requires `separate_turn_on_commands`.
data_description.include_config_in_attributes: Exposes all configuration values as attributes on the switch entity, visible in Home Assistant developer tools.
data_description.enable_diagnostic_sensors: Creates a diagnostic sensor per light showing its current adaptation status (active, blocked, manual override, error). Useful for troubleshooting why a light isn't adapting.

error.entity_missing: One or more selected light entities are missing from Home Assistant
error.brightness_range_invalid: Minimum brightness must be less than or equal to maximum brightness
error.color_temp_range_invalid: Minimum color temperature must be less than or equal to maximum color temperature
error.sunrise_range_invalid: Minimum sunrise time must be earlier than maximum sunrise time
error.sunset_range_invalid: Minimum sunset time must be earlier than maximum sunset time
error.requires_take_over_control: This option requires take_over_control to be enabled
error.requires_intercept: This option requires intercept to be enabled
error.requires_separate_turn_on: This option only applies when separate_turn_on_commands is enabled

apply.name: apply
apply.description: Applies the current Adaptive Lighting settings to lights.

entity_id.description: The `entity_id` of the switch with the settings to apply. 📝
entity_id.name: entity_id

lights.description: A light (or list of lights) to apply the settings to. 💡
lights.name: lights

transition.description: Duration of transition when lights change, in seconds. 🕑
transition.name: transition

adapt_brightness.description: Whether to adapt the brightness of the light. 🌞
adapt_brightness.name: adapt_brightness

adapt_color.description: Whether to adapt the color on supporting lights. 🌈
adapt_color.name: adapt_color

prefer_rgb_color.description: Whether to prefer RGB color adjustment over light color temperature when possible. 🌈
prefer_rgb_color.name: prefer_rgb_color

turn_on_lights.description: Whether to turn on lights that are currently off. 🔆
turn_on_lights.name: turn_on_lights

set_manual_control.name: set_manual_control
set_manual_control.description: Mark whether a light is 'manually controlled'.

entity_id.description: The `entity_id` of the switch in which to (un)mark the light as being `manually controlled`. 📝
entity_id.name: entity_id

lights.description: entity_id(s) of lights, if not specified, all lights in the switch are selected. 💡
lights.name: lights

manual_control.description: Whether to add ("true") or remove ("false") all adapted attributes of the light from the "manual_control" list, or the name of an attribute for selective addition. 🔒
manual_control.name: manual_control

change_switch_settings.name: change_switch_settings
change_switch_settings.description: Change any settings you'd like in the switch. All options here are the same as in the config flow.

use_defaults.description: Sets the default values not specified in this service call. Options: "current" (default, retains current values), "factory" (resets to documented defaults), or "configuration" (reverts to switch config defaults). ⚙️
use_defaults.name: use_defaults

include_config_in_attributes.description: Show all options as attributes on the switch in Home Assistant when set to `true`. 📝
include_config_in_attributes.name: include_config_in_attributes

turn_on_lights.description: Whether to turn on lights that are currently off. 🔆
turn_on_lights.name: turn_on_lights

initial_transition.description: Duration of the first transition when lights turn from `off` to `on` in seconds. ⏲️
initial_transition.name: initial_transition

sleep_transition.description: Duration of transition when "sleep mode" is toggled in seconds. 😴
sleep_transition.name: sleep_transition

only_once.description: Adapt lights only when they are turned on (`true`) or keep adapting them (`false`). 🔄
only_once.name: only_once

prefer_rgb_color.description: Whether to prefer RGB color adjustment over light color temperature when possible. 🌈
prefer_rgb_color.name: prefer_rgb_color

separate_turn_on_commands.description: Use separate `light.turn_on` calls for color and brightness, needed for some light types. 🔀
separate_turn_on_commands.name: separate_turn_on_commands

send_split_delay.description: Delay (ms) between `separate_turn_on_commands` for lights that don't support simultaneous brightness and color setting. ⏲️
send_split_delay.name: send_split_delay

sleep_brightness.description: Brightness percentage of lights in sleep mode. Set to 0 to prevent lights from turning on. 😴
sleep_brightness.name: sleep_brightness

sleep_rgb_or_color_temp.description: Use either `"rgb_color"` or `"color_temp"` in sleep mode. 🌙
sleep_rgb_or_color_temp.name: sleep_rgb_or_color_temp

sleep_rgb_color.description: RGB color in sleep mode (used when `sleep_rgb_or_color_temp` is "rgb_color"). 🌈
sleep_rgb_color.name: sleep_rgb_color

sleep_color_temp.description: Color temperature in sleep mode (used when `sleep_rgb_or_color_temp` is `color_temp`) in Kelvin. 😴
sleep_color_temp.name: sleep_color_temp

sunrise_offset.description: Adjust sunrise time with a positive or negative offset in seconds. ⏰
sunrise_offset.name: sunrise_offset

sunrise_time.description: Set a fixed time (HH:MM:SS) for sunrise. 🌅
sunrise_time.name: sunrise_time

sunset_offset.description: Adjust sunset time with a positive or negative offset in seconds. ⏰
sunset_offset.name: sunset_offset

sunset_time.description: Set a fixed time (HH:MM:SS) for sunset. 🌇
sunset_time.name: sunset_time

max_sunrise_time.description: Set the latest virtual sunrise time (HH:MM:SS), allowing for earlier sunrises. 🌅
max_sunrise_time.name: max_sunrise_time

min_sunset_time.description: Set the earliest virtual sunset time (HH:MM:SS), allowing for later sunsets. 🌇
min_sunset_time.name: min_sunset_time

take_over_control.description: Pause adaptation of individual lights and hand over (manual) control to other sources that issue `light.turn_on` calls for lights that are on. 🔒
take_over_control.name: take_over_control

take_over_control_mode.description: The adaptation pausing mode when other sources change brightness and/or color of lights. `pause_all` always pauses both brightness and color adaptation. `pause_changed` pauses the adaptation of only the changed attributes and continues adapting unchanged attributes, e.g., continues color adaptation when only brightness was changed.
take_over_control_mode.name: take_over_control_mode

detect_non_ha_changes.description: Detects and halts adaptations for non-`light.turn_on` state changes. Needs `take_over_control` enabled. 🕵️ Caution: ⚠️ Some lights might falsely indicate an 'on' state, which could result in lights turning on unexpectedly. Note that this calls `homeassistant.update_entity` every `interval`! Disable this feature if you encounter such issues.
detect_non_ha_changes.name: detect_non_ha_changes

adapt_delay.name: adapt_delay
